explainability.path_to_improvement: Document how explanations are provided to users affected by AI decisions.

external_accountability.findings: Shareholder proposals request independent assessments of AI risk management.
external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Publish third-party audit results, certifications, or regulatory compliance documentation.

relevant_sources.summary: This report on AI shareholder proposals provides evidence for **external accountability, governance, oversight, and transparency**. The proposals discussed seek transparency reports on company automation initiatives and disclosures on the impacts of advanced technologies on workers, supporting the **transparency** pillar. Furthermore, requests for dedicated board committees for AI risks, amendments to committee charters for AI oversight, and independent assessments of AI risk management all indicate a focus on **governance** and **oversight**. The mention of independent assessments also points to **external accountability**.
relevant_sources.title: Unveiling Key Trends in AI Shareholder Proposals

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: Ryder describes its strategic approach to AI, including investments in AI startups and partnerships, and references the establishment of a dedicated AI lab. A corporate sustainability report mentions "predictive analytics" and "AVT investments." Shareholder proposals also request dedicated board committees for AI risks, amendments to committee charters for AI oversight, and independent assessments of AI risk management.

relevant_sources.summary: This company webpage for "Ryder Technology & Innovations" provides evidence for the **governance** and **transparency** pillars of responsible AI. The document supports governance by describing Ryder's strategic approach to AI, including investments in AI startups and partnerships, and the establishment of a dedicated AI lab. Transparency is evidenced by the company's stated intent to embed AI into proprietary platforms and leverage AI for customer-facing technologies and logistics optimization, implying a commitment to making AI integration and its use cases visible.
relevant_sources.title: Ryder Technology & Innovations

relevant_sources.summary: The Ryder 2024 Corporate Sustainability Report provides evidence for the **governance** pillar of responsible AI. The report's mention of "predictive analytics" and "AVT investments" suggests the implementation of AI-driven systems and strategic oversight of these technologies, indicating a governance framework is in place.

oversight.findings: Shareholder proposals request dedicated board committees for AI risks and amendments to committee charters for AI oversight. These proposals also seek independent assessments of AI risk management.
oversight.path_to_improvement: Publish override mechanisms, escalation processes, or appeal workflows.

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: Ryder states its intent to embed AI into proprietary platforms and leverage AI for customer-facing technologies and logistics optimization. A company blog post details specific AI and ML applications within the supply chain, explaining system capabilities, data inputs, and how models learn. Shareholder proposals also seek transparency reports on company automation initiatives and disclosures on the impacts of advanced technologies on workers.

relevant_sources.summary: This company-owned blog post, "Ryder: AI in the Supply Chain," provides evidence for the **transparency** pillar of responsible AI. The blog post details Ryder's specific AI and ML applications within the supply chain, including driver index generation, market rate prediction, risk management, and natural language processing for data analysis. By explaining system capabilities, data inputs, and how models learn, the source demonstrates transparency about its AI systems and their functions.
relevant_sources.title: Ryder: AI in the Supply Chain

summary.overall_findings: Ryder System states its intent to embed AI into proprietary platforms and leverage it for customer-facing technologies, reflecting documented practices in transparency. Its strategic approach to AI, including investments in AI startups and partnerships, is described under governance. At the policy level, shareholder proposals request dedicated board committees for AI risks under oversight, and independent assessments of AI risk management are requested for external accountability. These materials indicate that 4 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars have documented evidence. No qualifying public evidence was found for fairness, explainability, and 1 additional pillar, based on a review of 14 publicly available sources.
